Delivered POC Details to Karen and Team on 31st March - Tracked in April - Karen P. - OOO Mid April POC for ANA and Spend was positive - need to connect on collab for Agentic Strat Sourcing Remaining need from Ronit / Pilots is optimal categories for iSource. Finish Deck and reach out to Karen / force the Eduardo connection. Need Zycus Preferred / Safe MRO or Indirect Categories for a. Have a target score card in mind (already drafted) Parallel - Track Eduardo Viera - Indirect Procurement - need a Jolt to action Call set with Karen on 24th February to dive deep into business plan. i may "conveniently" appear in in Cary NC to do an onsite - will test that on 17th after the presidents Day break. - Also have pinged Eduardo for Pinnacle attendance - he deferred and I've shared Hackett/Broadridge update. Post Conf Call on 28th - Collaborate on Deere Specific Busienss Case - Verified path to agreement is through Karen Pagoreak and Eduardo Viera Timing for close is now at risk for Q4.
hard.ais_why: • On the 12/18/2025 call, champion Karen Pagorek pushed Arthur to define autonomous and agentic terms more precisely, specifically asking him to disentangle e-auctions, tactical spend, and autonomous sourcing with negotiation inside iSource. That terminology pressure is substantive AI dialogue from the buyer side, not surface-level interest. • Karen then asked what a pilot might look like. Arthur proposed pilots covering iSource, autonomous sourcing, and spend analysis: a sandbox RFX event, an MRO category negotiation, and a 3-month spend cube prototype. Karen also pressure-tested ROI realism on the $48M savings claim, calling for transparency on the assumptions. • The offsetting signal is that the three-prong AI POC was paused on 5/11/26. That is a real down-signal post-call. The buyer is still at "quantify incremental value" and remembers poor feedback from past supplier evaluations. Active autonomous-sourcing dialogue offsets the pause, but the pause is why this stays in the cautious band.
hard.primary_competitor: Unnamed second I/O vendor (intake, sourcing and spend; deep-dive since 11 May 2026)

north_star_verdict.headline: John Deere paused the Zycus POC on 11 May to deep-dive an unnamed rival vendor for two months; Eduardo Viera confirmed on 12 Jun that Zycus is still an option, and the buyer-accepted 20 Jul call with Karen Pagorek decides whether the evaluation restarts.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-06-06 (398 days old). Close 2027-02-22 is 228 days out. Shortlisted since 2025-12-05 (216 days in stage; submission-date fields are unpopulated, so the stage series is read from field history). Close date pushed three times in 2026: on 2026-02-26 (27 Mar to 26 Jun), 2026-05-04 (to 30 Sep) and 2026-05-11 (to 22 Feb 2027), a cumulative slip of about 11 months. Buyer-imposed standoff runs 11 May to 20 Jul while they evaluate a second vendor. Pace required to hold 22 Feb 2027: restart the evaluation on the 20 Jul call, complete the resumed pilots by end September, run commercials and ROI validation October to November, and contract December to January. If the 20 Jul call does not restart the evaluation, the close moves past Q1 FY27.
evidence: Karen Pagorek paused the three-prong POC on 11 May 2026: 'Karen Pagorek shared that they are re-assessing priorities and that the POC will not move forward' (SF Next_Step__c, 11 May 2026).

The buyer is deep-diving a second vendor covering intake, sourcing and spend on a self-set two-month window: 'John Deere is evaluating a second vendor - for intake, sourcing and spend - will complete eval in 2 months - check in on July 20th' (SF task, 11 May 2026).
Eduardo Viera, 12 Jun 2026 email: 'We are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those. Karen will be sharing more details in your next call.' Zycus is still in, but not stated as leading.
Best Case is not defensible while the POC is paused, a rival holds the buyer's attention, and no commercial terms exist; the honest category is Pipeline until the 20 Jul call restarts the evaluation.
Trajectory versus the prior sweep: holding steady (no new buyer action since the 12 Jun email). The single biggest blocker to close remains the paused POC pending the rival deep-dive outcome.

metrics.narrative: The pain is concrete but the numbers are still Zycus's, not Deere's. Karen Pagorek reviewed the December proposal's claimed gains (40% sourcing cycle-time reduction, 60% enhanced bids, 3x faster stakeholder engagement) and pushed back: 'that seems pretty optimistic... either to influence my thinking that, oh, that actually is attainable, or I need to reset' (Avoma, 18 Dec 2025), and she pressure-tested the $48M compliance-savings figure, which Arthur himself offered to re-baseline to $10M to $12M for Deere's maturity. Arthur offered the 70-to-80-data-point value-engineering benchmark exercise on that call, but nothing shows it ever ran, and the AIS read confirms the buyer is still at 'quantify incremental value' (AIS_Why, referencing the 11 May 2026 pause). April 2026 tracking logged 'POC for ANA and Spend was positive' (SF Next_Step__c), so proof exists in fragments; converting it into Deere-validated numbers before the 20 Jul call is the ranked move.

economic_buyer.narrative: Eduardo Viera (Global Director, Supply Management Indirect Materials and Services; primary Executive Sponsor contact role) is the working economic buyer: he secured the program's funding and owns its justification, 'One was lack of funding, and thankfully, last year in '24, we secured funding... we are backing this investment with... what we call hard savings or hard numbers' (Avoma, 30 Sep 2025). His engagement is real but thin: after the 30 Sep 1:1 he went quiet for months, then replied personally on 12 Jun 2026 that 'We are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those.' CFO Joshua Jepsen holds an Executive Sponsor contact role but has never appeared on any call or thread, so the approval chain above Eduardo is unmapped. The ranked move is converting Eduardo's 12 Jun reply into a post-20-Jul make-versus-buy working session.

decision_criteria.narrative: The operative criteria are visible across three calls even though no formal criteria document exists. Eduardo Viera framed it as make-versus-buy complementarity: 'if there is a solution working already in the marketplace that we don't need to reinvent, let's try to see how we can adapt that', with modular pick-and-choose of RFX and e-auctions to fill portfolio gaps (Avoma, 30 Sep 2025). Karen Pagorek added evidence-based ROI (benchmarks, references, her 'pretty optimistic' challenge) and pilot-before-purchase validation (Avoma, 18 Dec 2025), and Karen Powers set configurability to Deere processes: 'we like to customize our tools to match our processes... we call it deorizing' (Avoma, 24 Jun 2025). What remains unknown is the criteria sheet governing the current two-vendor comparison; Karen Pagorek owes that context at the 20 Jul call.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer runs an informal, buyer-controlled evaluation rather than an RFP: demo (24 Jun 2025), exec 1:1 (30 Sep 2025), budgetary-proposal review (18 Dec 2025), business-plan deep-dive (24 Feb 2026), POV proposal to a six-person team (31 Mar 2026), positive internal feedback on ANA and Spend (April), then a unilateral pause to deep-dive a second vendor (11 May 2026). The rep's verified read stands: 'Verified path to agreement is through Karen Pagorek and Eduardo Viera' (SF Next_Step__c, Feb 2026). Eduardo said on 30 Sep 2025 'I don't think that we are looking at a competitive [tender], because we are very concentrated on what we are doing now', yet a second vendor entered by May 2026, so the process has hardened into a comparison Zycus does not control. Funding was secured in 2024 under Eduardo; the approval chain beyond him (CFO Joshua Jepsen) is unmapped.
sources: SF Next_Step__c and Next Step history

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are on record: no MSA, SOW, InfoSec, legal or procurement-channel discussion has occurred, and the December proposal was explicitly 'not anywhere near a final commercial proposal... very much budgetary estimate' (Arthur, Avoma 18 Dec 2025). NDAs are in place ('we've got all the NDAs and the like', same call). This is normal for a Shortlisted deal that has not re-entered active evaluation. Visibility gets created by the 20 Jul outcome and a resumed pilot with a mutual plan; pilot SOW scoping would then be the first paper step.
sources: Avoma call 18 Dec 2025 (Arthur Raguette, Karen Pagorek)

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed, multi-voiced and owned. Karen Powers: quoting runs on a homegrown TCS tool plus Excel with no bid packages, and the customize-everything culture ('we don't call it customizing, we call it deorizing') slows AI transformation, which is why they are 'looking a lot more heavily in the market' (Avoma, 24 Jun 2025). Eduardo Viera: manual work and organizational drag, with the funded program justified on 'hard savings or hard numbers... incremental cost reduction, some organizational optimization... the elimination of manual work' (Avoma, 30 Sep 2025). Karen Pagorek engaged on maverick-spend compliance and prior vendor disappointment; Arthur recapped, unchallenged, that their previous spend-analysis vendor left them unhappy 'with the accuracy or the turnaround time' (Avoma, 18 Dec 2025).

champion.narrative: Karen Pagorek (Global Strategic Sourcing Manager, Evaluator contact role) is the only working champion and she is cooling. What developed: she drove the 18 Dec 2025 proposal review 1:1, co-designed the three pilot prongs, said 'I plan to discuss a little further here with Eduardo on some of these pilot proof of concept type options', and scheduled the 31 Mar 2026 POV call with five colleagues. What is missing or went backward: she paused the POC on 11 May 2026 ('re-assessing priorities'), and the rep still has to 'force the Eduardo connection' (SF Next_Step__c), so she is not escalating for Zycus at power level. Counter-signals keep her warm: she attended the Zycus Agentic AI summit on 13 May mid-pause (email, 21 May 2026) and suggested then accepted the 20 Jul reconnect. No political champion exists; Karen Powers (tech-stack owner, dormant since December 2025) is the natural second build.

competition.narrative: The field, ranked by current threat: (1) an unnamed second 'I/O' vendor covering intake, sourcing and spend, deep-dived 11 May to 20 Jul 2026 ('Doiing a deep dive on another eternal I/O provider. Did not indicate which one', SF Next_Step__c 11 May 2026), with Eduardo Viera's 12 Jun email confirming a live options evaluation; (2) in-house build / do-nothing: homegrown TCS quoting plus the iProcure build with OpenAI, Ultrametrics and Worldlink partnerships (Karen Powers, Avoma 24 Jun 2025), 'in-house build' is a named ramification of the pause, and Deere's SAP IT team 'are going to look at us as competition in a major way' (Arthur to Eduardo, acknowledged, Avoma 30 Sep 2025); (3) GEP, last named Feb 2026 ('Pilot Spend Analysis to counter GEP', SF Next_Step__c); (4) Oracle and SAP Ariba sit in Competitors__c with no call evidence and are dormant. The wedge: 20 years of procurement-AI depth and agentic breadth (autonomous sourcing plus Merlin intake) against a narrower orchestration rival, positioned as accelerating Deere's own roadmap rather than replacing it.

deal_movement.summary: The deal moved backward in May and has idled since. The three-prong POC (proposed 31 Mar 2026, positive April feedback on ANA and Spend) was paused on 11 May when Karen Pagorek said priorities are being re-assessed and a second vendor is being deep-dived, and the close date slipped twice inside that same week to 22 Feb 2027. The pulse since is thin but real: Karen attended the Zycus Agentic AI summit on 13 May, Eduardo Viera confirmed on 12 Jun that Zycus is still one of the options, and Karen accepted the 20 Jul touch base. Rep outreach beyond that thread (Gia Duke, Kathleen Johnson, the 11 Jun blog email) has gone unanswered.

items.change: Close date pushed from 26 Jun to 30 Sep 2026 (second push of 2026)
Karen Pagorek paused the three-prong POC ('re-assessing priorities'); a second I/O vendor deep-dive began with a two-month window; close pushed from 30 Sep 2026 to 22 Feb 2027
Karen Pagorek suggested and accepted the 20 Jul reconnect to share the evaluation outcome
Karen Pagorek attended the Zycus Agentic AI Procurement summit during the pause
Eduardo Viera replied that Zycus remains one of the evaluated options and Karen will share details on the next call (closes the prior engage-the-sponsor move)
Outbound calls to Gia Duke (twice, 3 Jun) and Kathleen Johnson (2 Jul, Horizon call cycle) could not connect

day_summary.overall: The most recent day of real deal activity was 12 June, when the status-check thread with the primary sponsor came back. Eduardo Viera replied to Arthur's update, confirming that John Deere is evaluating options, that Zycus is one of them, and that Karen Pagorek will share more detail at the July call. Arthur thanked him the same day and reaffirmed Zycus's interest in engaging openly. Since then the deal has been quiet apart from unanswered call attempts, holding for the buyer-accepted 20 July touch base.

items.name: Eduardo Viera's reply on the evaluation
items.summary: Eduardo confirmed Zycus is one of the options being evaluated and said Karen will share details at the next call.

items.name: Arthur's same-day thanks to Eduardo
items.summary: Arthur thanked Eduardo for the transparency and reaffirmed Zycus's appreciation for the open engagement.

critical_signals.text: John Deere paused the Zycus POC on 11 May to spend two months deep-diving an unnamed rival covering intake, sourcing and spend, with an in-house build named as a possible outcome.
The last real exchange was Eduardo Viera's 12 June reply confirming Zycus is still one of the options and that Karen Pagorek will share the assessment details on the accepted 20 July call.
Budget exists on Eduardo Viera's hard-savings mandate, but Karen Pagorek called our claimed gains optimistic in December and the value case has never been quantified in John Deere's own numbers.

competitive_position.summary: Ranked by current threat: the unnamed second 'I/O' vendor is the single strongest current threat, holding the buyer's exclusive attention 11 May to 20 Jul 2026 (Eduardo Viera confirmed a live options evaluation on 12 Jun). In-house build / do-nothing is the standing structural rival: Deere's homegrown TCS and iProcure stack with OpenAI, Ultrametrics and Worldlink partnerships, reinforced by a 'deorizing' culture and an SAP IT team that views Zycus as competition; 'in-house build' was explicitly named as a possible outcome of the pause. GEP is a medium, receding threat, last named Feb 2026 as the target of the counter-positioning spend pilot. Oracle and SAP Ariba appear only in the Competitors__c field with no 2025-2026 call evidence and are dormant.

competitors.how_we_win: Identify the vendor, then contrast agentic execution depth (autonomous sourcing, ANA, Merlin) against intake-only orchestration on the 20 Jul call.

competitors.how_we_win: Sell Eduardo's own 30 Sep 2025 frame: adapt what already works in the market instead of reinventing it; position every pilot as accelerating the iProcure roadmap.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: Pilot Spend Analysis to counter GEP
competitors.how_we_win: Land the already-positive ANA and Spend pilot results as Deere-validated numbers before GEP re-enters the conversation.

competitors.name: Oracle
competitors.source: SF Competitors__c (no call or Next Step evidence)
competitors.how_we_win: No active fight; keep the agentic-depth narrative current so a legacy-suite re-entry finds no opening.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.source: SF Competitors__c (no call or Next Step evidence)
competitors.how_we_win: Deere runs SAP ERP and its IT team is defensive; win by integrating cleanly (iSaaS connectors) rather than fighting the ERP.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Deere's stated buying criteria: complement the in-house build with modular pieces, prove value in scoped pilots before purchase, validate ROI against their own baselines, respect the configure-to-Deere culture, and protect supplier relationships in autonomous negotiation. Zycus is aligned on modularity and pilot design, and exposed on quantified proof; the buy-versus-build reflex is the standing headwind.

items.criterion: Modular, pick-and-choose architecture that complements the in-house iProcure build
items.quote: we don't have RFX. Well, how can we connect that with this, or e-auctions or whatever the case might be in your portfolio and what pieces of that we could pick and complement what we are working on

items.criterion: Proof of value in scoped pilots before any purchase decision
items.quote: I plan to discuss a little further here with Eduardo on some of these pilot proof of concept type options

items.criterion: ROI claims validated against Deere's own baselines, not vendor benchmarks
items.quote: that seems pretty optimistic, and so I'm thinking I'd like to hear more either to influence my thinking that, oh, that actually is attainable, or or I need to reset

items.criterion: Configurability to Deere processes without heavy customization (the 'deorizing' culture)
items.quote: historically, Deere has definitely been one where we like to customize our tools to match our processes versus purchasing a solution and modifying our processes... we don't call it customizing, we call it deorizing

items.criterion: Supplier experience in autonomous negotiation must beat their past poor evaluations
items.quote: we have done some evaluations with that, and and we haven't gotten great feedback in in all areas

items.requirement: Hold the two-month evaluation check-in Karen requested; she accepted the 20 Jul meeting

items.area: Approval chain above Eduardo Viera (CFO Joshua Jepsen never engaged; sign-off path for a $450k commitment unknown)
Stage submission-date fields (Qualified/Formal Eval/Shortlisted submission dates) unpopulated; stage velocity read from field history instead
Competitors__c reads 'Oracle;SAP Ariba;Others' while the live rivals are the unnamed I/O vendor, internal build and GEP

best_practice_check.summary: Three levers decide this deal: proof-led credibility (the buyer distrusts vendor ROI claims), positioning Zycus as an accelerator of Deere's build culture rather than its replacement, and widening power access beyond a single cooling evaluator.
flags: Buyer trusts proof, not claims: Karen challenged our ROI as optimistic, so anchor every future touch in Deere-validated numbers, references and benchmarks.
Do-nothing is the deepest rival: Deere's deorizing build culture and SAP IT resistance mean every proposal must accelerate the iProcure roadmap, never replace it.
No political champion: Karen Pagorek evaluates but cannot or will not escalate; develop Karen Powers (tech-stack owner, dormant since December) as the agentic-roadmap advocate.

items.title: Global Director, Supply Management Indirect Materials and Services
items.risk: Controls the funding he secured in 2024 on a hard-savings case, but engages rarely: one 1:1 (30 Sep 2025) and one email (12 Jun 2026) in nine months; not yet sponsoring Zycus against the rival deep-dive.

champion_strength.summary: Karen Pagorek is the only live champion and she is cooling: she designed the pilot scopes, pressure-tested our definitions and ROI (18 Dec 2025), and carried the deal through March, but she paused the POC on 11 May 2026, is running a rival deep-dive, and the Eduardo connection still has to be forced by the rep rather than brokered by her. Offsetting warmth: she attended the Zycus Agentic AI summit on 13 May and suggested then accepted the 20 Jul reconnect. No political champion exists at power level.
champion_strength.source: Avoma 18 Dec 2025 + SF Next_Step__c 11 May 2026 + email 21 May 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The agentic story is the deal's spine and it has landed: Karen Pagorek pushed for precise autonomous-sourcing definitions and co-designed three AI pilots (18 Dec 2025), April feedback on the ANA and Spend POC was positive, and she attended the Agentic AI summit mid-pause (13 May 2026). What is missing is not positioning but quantified proof tied to Deere's own numbers, and a counter to the rival vendor now carrying an intake-and-orchestration story of its own.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 / Strong is the field read and the calls corroborate it: substantive buyer-side AI dialogue (terminology pressure, pilot design, ROI realism testing on the $48M claim) rather than surface interest, on top of Deere's own OpenAI and Ultrametrics partnerships. The 11 May POC pause tempers the signal but does not reverse it; Karen still attended the 13 May Agentic AI summit during the pause.

items.detail: Buyer 're-assessing priorities' paused the three-prong POC before value was quantified; evaluation attention moved to a rival for two months, with 'on hold, in-house build, or competitive loss' the rep's own framing of the possible outcomes.
items.source: Karen Pagorek email 4 May 2026 + SF Next_Step__c 11 May 2026

items.detail: Close date pushed three times in 2026 (27 Mar to 26 Jun to 30 Sep to 22 Feb 2027) while the deal sits 216 days in Shortlisted; the 20 Jul outcome decides whether 22 Feb 2027 is credible.

CFO Joshua Jepsen (Executive Sponsor contact role) has never engaged; executive sponsorship rests solely on Eduardo Viera, who has touched the deal twice in nine months (30 Sep 2025 call, 12 Jun 2026 email).

Deere's internal SAP IT team 'are going to look at us as competition in a major way' (Arthur, acknowledged by Eduardo Viera), and the homegrown iProcure/TCS 'deorizing' culture structurally favors building over buying.
items.source: Avoma call 30 Sep 2025 + Avoma demo 24 Jun 2025 (Karen Powers)

items.detail: ROI claims challenged as 'pretty optimistic' by Karen Pagorek and the 70-to-80-data-point value-engineering benchmark was offered but never executed, leaving the $450k ask unjustified in Deere's own numbers.

confidence_signals.summary: Cooling since 4 May 2026: the buyer paused the POC, imposed a two-month standoff, and outreach beyond Karen goes unanswered. Offsetting: Eduardo Viera's 12 Jun confirmation that Zycus is still in, Karen's summit attendance on 13 May, and the buyer-accepted 20 Jul reconnect.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: John Deere paused the Zycus POC on 11 May to deep-dive an unnamed rival for two months; Eduardo Viera confirms Zycus is still an option, and the buyer-accepted 20 Jul call with Karen Pagorek decides whether the evaluation restarts or drifts to the rival or an in-house build.

win_position.text: Zycus is one of the options, not the leader: John Deere paused our POC on 11 May 2026 to deep-dive an unnamed rival across intake, sourcing and spend, and Eduardo Viera's 12 Jun email confirms a live multi-vendor evaluation.

The agentic story has landed with the buyer: Karen Pagorek co-designed three AI pilots and pressure-tested autonomous-sourcing definitions on 18 Dec 2025, April tracking logged 'POC for ANA and Spend was positive', and AIS reads 6.0 Strong.
The economic-buyer door is open: Eduardo Viera funded the program on hard savings ('last year in '24, we secured funding', 30 Sep 2025) and replied personally on 12 Jun that Zycus remains an option.

The value case is unproven in Deere's numbers: Karen Pagorek called the claimed gains 'pretty optimistic' on 18 Dec 2025, the offered benchmark exercise never ran, and the deal stalled at 'quantify incremental value'.
Power coverage is one cooling thread: CFO Joshua Jepsen has never engaged, and June-July outreach to Gia Duke and Kathleen Johnson went unanswered while the close date slipped three times in 2026.

deal_momentum.text: The buyer imposed a standoff on 11 May 2026: the POC is paused and Karen Pagorek asked Zycus to wait two months while the rival evaluation runs, so no buyer forward action has occurred in the last 14 days.
A buyer-owned milestone anchors the deal: Karen Pagorek suggested the touch base herself and accepted the 20 Jul meeting to share the evaluation outcome.
The thread is alive across channels: Eduardo Viera replied on 12 Jun that Zycus is still being evaluated, and Karen attended the Zycus Agentic AI summit on 13 May during the pause.
Everything else is one-way: calls to Gia Duke (3 Jun, twice) and Kathleen Johnson (2 Jul) could not connect and the 11 Jun agentic-blogs email drew no reply.

customer_commitment.text: Karen Pagorek stepped back on 4 May 2026 ('we're reviewing and assessing a range of alternatives') and paused the POC a week later, citing a priorities re-assessment.

Commitment has not gone to zero: Karen chose to attend the Zycus Agentic AI summit on 13 May, set the 20 Jul reconnect herself, and Eduardo Viera confirmed on 12 Jun that Zycus remains under evaluation.
No buyer resources are committed to Zycus work since the April POC feedback; the next commitment test is whether the 20 Jul call produces a dated restart plan.

deal_risk.text: The rep's own Next Step names the downside outcomes of the pause: 'on hold, in-house build, or competitive loss' (11 May 2026), and the rival's identity is still unknown.

Deere's build culture is a structural threat: the SAP IT team 'are going to look at us as competition in a major way' (30 Sep 2025) and the homegrown iProcure/TCS stack gives do-nothing a working alternative.
The $450k ask rests on a hard-savings mandate owned by Eduardo Viera, yet the CFO has never engaged and Zycus's ROI claims were challenged as optimistic and never re-proven.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer paused our POC to deep-dive a rival first; Eduardo Viera (12 Jun 2026): 'We are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those', in the field but not leading.

champion.evidence: Karen Pagorek cooled: paused the POC 11 May citing a priorities re-assessment, yet kept the 20 Jul door open and attended the 13 May summit.

exec_access.evidence: Eduardo Viera (funding owner) replied personally on 12 Jun 2026; direct face time limited to one 1:1 on 30 Sep 2025; CFO never engaged.

competitive.evidence: An unnamed rival holds the buyer's exclusive attention 11 May to 20 Jul 2026, with in-house build named as a possible outcome (SF Next_Step__c).

scope_change.from: Full source-to-pay plus agentic suite (12 products, $450k) at creation
scope_change.to: Unchanged product scope and amount as of 9 Jul 2026
scope_change.detail: Products__c and the $450k amount are unchanged since creation; the March 2026 three-prong POC (ANA, Spend, iSource) narrowed validation focus as sequencing, not a scope cut. Watch the 20 Jul call for a modular carve-out if the buyer returns wanting fewer pieces, which would be the defensive signal.

customer_preference.evidence: Eduardo Viera, 12 Jun 2026 email: 'We are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those'; Karen Pagorek paused the Zycus POC on 11 May 2026 to deep-dive another vendor first.

business_case.evidence: Pain and a hard-savings funding mandate are confirmed (Eduardo Viera, 30 Sep 2025: funding 'backed... with hard savings or hard numbers') and April 2026 ANA/Spend POC feedback was positive, but Karen Pagorek challenged Zycus's claimed gains as 'pretty optimistic' (18 Dec 2025) and no Deere-validated quantification exists.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: activities last 120 days; field history trailing 365 days; contact roles; Next_Step__c and history
gaps: Identity of the second 'I/O' vendor John Deere is deep-diving (named 11 May 2026) is unknown; the buyer declined to say which one
No transcript exists for the 11 May 2026 pause call, the 24 Feb 2026 business-plan call or the 31 Mar 2026 POV readout; those interactions are read from Next_Step__c, task summaries and emails only
Exact April 2026 POC feedback wording is unavailable (logged only as 'POC for ANA and Spend was positive' in Next_Step__c)
